data.title: China's Baidu plans to unveil AI model Ernie 4.5 on March 16 (Update)
data.description:
Update: The story was updated with a statement from Baidu.

Chinese internet search giant Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) plans to launch the next generation of its AI model, Ernie 4.5, with improved capabilities on March 16, the company said in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha.

The Ernie 4.5 model will have improved capabilities in areas like reasoning and feature enhanced multimodal capabilities.

Multimodal AI refers to AI systems which are capable of processing and integrating information from different types of data such as text, video, images, and audio.

Baidu will open-source the ERNIE 4.5 model on June 30. The company will also provide its AI chatbot Ernie Bot free to all users starting April 1.

"ERNIE 4.5 will be our best model ever. And we would like to have our users and customers try them out more easily than before. So our decision to open source ERNIE 4.5 series, is also backed by our deep confidence in our technology leadership," CEO Robin Li had said during the company's fourth quarter earnings call on Feb. 18.

Earlier this month, it was reported that the company plans to launch AI model Ernie 5 in the second half of this year.

Baidu's efforts comes on the heels of Chinese AI startup DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) unveiling its AI model R1 last month and detailing in a paper how large language models, or LLMs, could be built on tight budgets and improved without human supervision. U.S. tech stocks plunged on Jan. 27, with Nvidia seeing around $589B in market cap erased on that day.

Li noted during the earnings call that one that thing the company learned from DeepSeek was that open sourcing the best models can greatly help adoption. "When the model is open source, people naturally want to try it out of curiosity, which helps drive broader adoption," Li added.

Earlier this month, domestic AI rival Alibaba (BABA) released a new family of AI models called Qwen2.5-VL. Meanwhile TikTok parent ByteDance (BDNCE) has its chatbot Doubao.

Baidu was among the first companies in China to launch its AI service in 2023 after Microsoft-backed OpenAI unveiled ChatGPT in late 2022. In November 2024, Li noted that the company's AI capabilities are gaining broader market recognition, due to the increasing adoption of its AI model Ernie.

Earlier this month, Li noted that investment in data centers and cloud infrastructure is still required in spite of the cost efficiency shown by DeepSeek compared to the large AI models. Li also said that "in China, because of the constraints of compute, we have to innovate to reduce the cost both on the inference side and the training side."

data.title: Nordic American Tankers GAAP EPS of $0.01 misses by $0.02, revenue of $46.38M misses by $5M
data.description:
• Nordic American Tankers press release (NYSE:NAT): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.01 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $46.38M (-21.7% Y/Y) misses by $5M.

data.title: Técnicas Reunidas, S.A. reports FY results
data.description:
• Técnicas Reunidas, S.A. press release (OTCPK:TNISF): FY net profit at €89 million (+50% vs. 2023)
  • EBIT at €181 million (+16% vs. 2023) and 2024 EBIT margin at 4.1%.
  • Sales at €4,451 million (+8% vs. 2023), Q4 2024 sales at €1,232 million (+39% vs. Q4 2023)
  • FY Backlog at €12.5 billion (+9% vs. 2023) Q4 2024 EBIT at €50 million (+35% vs. Q4 2023)
  • Net cash position of €394 million at the end of December 2024

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Gold poised for second-straight monthly gain, oil faces 6th weekly decline
data.description:
Gold prices headed for a second-consecutive monthly gain on Friday, with analysts at BMI considering the U.S. dollar strength as the only bearish factor for gold in 2025.

Spot gold eased -0.47% to $2,862 an ounce on Friday, on its way to a 2.4% weekly fall - its first since December. The metal was, however, set for a second-straight monthly gain.

BMI believes that the rest of the factors will outweigh the impact of dollar strength, and gold will once again be an outperformer within the wider metals complex, a scenario that would be put into limbo only if the U.S. Fed proceeds to raise interest rates instead.

Looking ahead to the rest of 2025, BMI maintains the view that although short-term upward pressures on metal prices are likely in response to Trump-led trade policy shifts, the longer-term outlook remains bearish, as tariffs are expected to weigh on the market, pushing up the U.S. dollar and posing headwinds to metals, given their inverse relationship with the greenback.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Looking closer at individual metals' performance, copper led the base metals higher, rising by 1.4% m-o-m to $9,402/tonne as of February 25.

Commodity sub-asset classes showed significant price strength in 2025 thus far, with the Bloomberg Commodity Index gaining 4.9% in the year-to-date as of February 27.

Meanwhile, in the energy complex, oil prices were set for a sixth consecutive weekly fall.

Crude oil benchmarks Nymex crude (CL1:COM) was down 0.5% for the week and Brent (CO1:COM) 1.6%.

BMI maintained its view for Brent crude to average $76/bbl in 2025, down from $80/bbl in 2024, while acknowledging wide-ranging risks to the outlook, both to the upside and the down.

The brokerage expects significant volatility in pricing over the coming weeks and months as markets scramble to weigh the impact of Trump’s new policy positions, not least regarding tariff measures.

The net impact, however, will likely be bearish, given their potentially adverse effects on the global economy, BMI added.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Lxiang Education regains compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• Lxiang Education (NASDAQ:LXEH) said it has received a letter from Nasdaq stating that the firm had regained compliance with the minimum market value of publicly held shares rule. 
  • Press Release

data.title: Acerinox, S.A. reports FY results
data.description:
• Acerinox, S.A. press release (OTCPK:ANIOY): FY Profit for the year amounted to €225M.
  • EBITDA, €500M, was 29% lower than that of 2023, with a sales margin of 9%
  • The Group’s revenue during the fiscal year amounted to €5,413M (18% lower than that of 2023).

data.title: bpost NV/SA reports Q4 results
data.description:
• bpost NV/SA press release (OTCPK:BPOSF): Q4 Group operating income at €1,335M, +9.7% or +€117.8M compared to last year, including 214.1 mEUR contribution from Staci which has been consolidated as of August 1, 2024.
  • Group adjusted EBIT at €84M (margin of 6.3%) versus last year at 74.1 mEUR.
  • Group reported EBIT at -€222.9M, down by -€293.8M compared to last year.

data.title: Proximus PLC reports Q4 results
data.description:

  • Revenue of €1.69B (+10.5% Y/Y).
